question: A: "The phone is ringing!" B: "I ___ it."
explanation: Decisão tomada **na hora de falar** → **will**. "Going to" seria para um plano decidido antes.

question: Look at those dark clouds! It ___ soon.
explanation: Previsão com **evidência visível** (as nuvens estão ali) → **going to**. "Will" seria para opinião sem evidência.

question: We ___ a party next Saturday. We've already sent the invitations.
explanation: Plano **decidido antes** (os convites já foram enviados) → **are going to have**.

question: I promise I ___ you back tomorrow.
explanation: **Promessa** → will: "I promise I **will pay** you back".

question: Choose the correct sentence:
explanation: **Will + verbo base**, sem "to" e sem -ing. E "going to" precisaria do am/is/are.

question: She ___ a doctor. She starts medical school in September.
explanation: Intenção/plano já definido (ela já vai começar a faculdade) → **is going to be**. Não esqueça o verbo to be antes de "going to".

question: We ___ out tonight; we've decided to stay home.
explanation: Decisão tomada **antes** ("we've decided") → going to; na negativa: **aren't going to go**. "Won't to go" está errado (will não usa "to").

question: Your bags look heavy. I ___ you.
explanation: **Oferta de ajuda** feita na hora → **will help** ("I'll help you").

question: A: "Why are you wearing your running shoes?" B: "I ___ for a run."
explanation: Intenção já decidida (a pessoa já está com o tênis) → **am going to go**.

question: I'm sure you ___ the new movie. It's really funny.
explanation: Previsão baseada em **opinião** ("I'm sure") → **will love**.

question: Watch out! You ___ that glass!
explanation: Previsão com **evidência visível agora** (está prestes a acontecer) → **are going to knock over**.

question: A: "It's cold in here." B: "You're right — I ___ the heating on."
explanation: Decisão que **nasce na hora** da conversa → **will turn** ("I'll turn the heating on").

question: What ___ this weekend? Any plans?
explanation: Pergunta sobre **planos** → going to, com inversão correta: "What **are you going to** do?".

question: If you don't stop right now, I ___ your teacher!
explanation: Promessa/ameaça no momento da fala → **will + verbo base**: "I **will call** your teacher".

question: She's saving money because she ___ a new laptop.
explanation: **Intenção/plano prévio** (por isso ela já está economizando) → **is going to buy**.

question: Which sentence contains a mistake?
options: They're going to sell their house.
He will goes to the gym later.
explanation: Depois de **will**, o verbo fica na forma **base**: "He will **go**", nunca "will goes".

question: Maria has already enrolled: she ___ Italian classes next month.
explanation: Plano **já organizado antes** (ela já se matriculou) → **is going to take**.

question: Don't worry about the report. I ___ it before Friday — I promise.
explanation: **Promessa** → will + verbo base: "I **will finish** it".

question: A: "Did you know Sarah is in the hospital?" B: "No, I didn't! I ___ her this afternoon."
explanation: A pessoa **acabou de saber** da notícia, então a decisão nasceu agora → **will visit**. "Going to" indicaria um plano que já existia.

question: A: "Why did you buy so much flour?" B: "I ___ a cake for Tom's birthday."
explanation: A compra da farinha mostra uma **intenção prévia** → **am going to bake**. "Will" seria uma decisão tomada só agora.

question: The score is 4–0 and there are only five minutes left. Our team ___ .
explanation: Previsão com **evidência concreta agora** (o placar) → **is going to win**. "Will" seria só uma opinião, sem evidência.

question: Which sentence is appropriate in a formal email?
options: I'm gonna send the documents tomorrow.
I gonna send the documents tomorrow.
I will to send the documents tomorrow.
I will send the documents tomorrow.
explanation: **Gonna** é informal (fala) — evite na escrita formal. E "will" nunca leva "to": "I **will send**".

question: We ___ dinner with my parents on Sunday — it's all arranged. But I don't know what time we ___ back.
options: will have / are going to be
are going to have / will be
are going to have / are going to be
explanation: Plano combinado ("it's all arranged") → **are going to have**; previsão incerta, sem plano ("I don't know") → **will be**.
